text: AI hype continues to influence crypto and DeFi sectors, with comparisons drawn to the 2021 NFT hype cycle. New AI infrastructure layers and tools are emerging, such as HeyElsaAI for automated on-chain actions and Build4ai integration with Hyperliquid for autonomous trading agents. However, some caution is expressed about enterprise AI adoption pitfalls and the need for hands-on tech leadership to avoid hype-driven failures.
author: elfa_2048515162580377706

text: The HyperEVM ecosystem is gaining traction with multiple projects and NFTs launching on the platform. There is community discussion about identifying HyperEVM projects and the first NFT on Hyperliquid, TinyHyperCats, is considered a high risk/reward play. MegaETH ecosystem updates include migrations of projects like Monster and Xeet to MegaETH, and launches of new apps and tokens. However, there was a recent $1.5M hack on MegaETH/HyperEVM due to multisig access issues, highlighting security risks in the ecosystem.
author: elfa_2048519998453285167

text: Hyperliquid ($HYPE) continues to dominate the crypto perpetual futures market with strong fundamentals including high revenue buybacks (97%), growing open interest, and a unique ETF setup with no VC dilution. The protocol is expanding its asset universe through HIP-3, enabling permissionless perpetual markets on real-world assets (RWAs) and commodities, with $2.38B open interest on HIP-3 markets and $7.59B total perp OI. Whale activity shows large leveraged positions and strong conviction. The $HYPE token is viewed as undervalued with bullish price targets ranging from $50 to $150+. Hyperliquid is also leading in on-chain revenue, surpassing Ethereum in dApp revenue recently. Integration with AI agents and infrastructure upgrades (e.g., Build4ai) are underway to enable autonomous low-latency trading. The ecosystem includes NFTs like TinyHyperCats and Hypurr NFTs as rare assets. Overall, Hyperliquid is positioned as a key infrastructure player in the evolving decentralized finance and prediction markets landscape.
author: elfa_2048557739660685817

text: Highlight Clip Arthur Hayes: $15 Trillion in Credit Will Drive a $1 Million BTC On April 10, 2026, BitMEX founder Arthur Hayes spoke at Korea Blockchain Week, analyzing the potential money-printing trajectory of the Fed and the US Treasury. He expressed that due to fears of issuing long-term debt, the US government is utilizing the Genius Act and stablecoin regulations to find price-inelastic buyers for short-term Treasury bills. Hayes believes that if the government gains full control of monetary policy levers, it will lead to an estimated $15.2 trillion in credit expansion by 2028. He stated that this extreme macroeconomic easing will break the traditional four-year cycle and drive Bitcoin past the $1 million mark. — link

text: Highlight Clip US Admiral Backs Bitcoin: A Matter of National Interest In April 2026, Admiral Samuel Paparo, Commander of the U.S. Indo-Pacific Command, stated during a Senate Armed Services Committee hearing on the fiscal year 2027 defense budget request that Bitcoin can be regarded as a computer science tool integrating cryptography, blockchain and proof-of-work mechanisms. He pointed out that Bitcoin boasts enormous potential in this regard, and the costs brought by its proof-of-work protocols exceed those of basic algorithmic network security and operational maintenance. Beyond economic attributes, Bitcoin holds significant practical value in cybersecurity, and delivers positive implications for strengthening the overall national power of the United States. — link
